vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF for #642: tracking umbrella for PR #401 -- framework maintainability, rule ownership, and determinism codification

plan.title: tracking: framework maintainability, rule ownership, and determinism codification (PR #401)

narratives.Problem: PR #401 ("feat(coding): codebase hygiene, DDD framework structure, and determinism design") is broad but not anchored to a directive issue. Without a backlog/roadmap reference the work is harder to discuss, refine, or split. The PR is too large to be a single leaf issue but the arc of work is coherent and deserves an umbrella anchor.
narratives.Overview: Create and maintain a single tracking issue (this one, #642) that anchors the current intended scope of PR #401. Treat it as an umbrella, not a leaf: if implementation reveals cleaner separations, follow-up issues can be split out and linked here.
narratives.Background: PR #401 spans hygiene codification, language-specific hygiene guidance, framework structure / rule ownership, review-fix correctness cleanup, and determinism design documentation. It currently lacks a directive issue to reference as `Refs #<n>`.
narratives.Constraint: PR #401 should reference this tracking issue with `Refs #642` (not `Closes`) unless the merged PR fully satisfies the entire tracked scope; the umbrella must remain open across any future splits.
narratives.Risk: Without enrichment this vBRIEF stays a thin shell and cannot drive refinement. Splitting later without an umbrella causes loss of arc context; closing #642 prematurely on a partial merge orphans remaining work.
narratives.Outcome: Acceptance criteria: (1) a single directive issue anchors the current intended scope of PR #401; (2) the tracked work covers hygiene codification, rule ownership/framework structure, review-fix cleanup, and determinism design; (3) PR #401 references #642 as its backlog anchor; (4) if work is split, #642 remains the umbrella pointing at follow-up issues.
narratives.Test: Verification: PR #401 body contains `Refs #642`; #642 remains open until all five scope items are either merged or split into linked follow-up issues; vBRIEF items reflect the merged subset of work.

narrative.Action: Add `coding/hygiene.md` and strengthen hygiene-related guidance in shared coding/verification docs.
Extend consistent types / error-handling / dead-code guidance across the mainstream language docs.
Add `core/events.md`; strengthen `core/glossary.md` as the authoritative vocabulary; clarify ownership in `REFERENCES.md`; connect the structure back into `main.md`.
Fold in concrete corrections from PR #401 review on tooling claims and wording details.
Add `docs/more-determinism.md` and document where task-based enforcement should replace prose-only guidance over time.

x-tracking.split_policy: If implementation reveals cleaner separations, split into follow-up issues and link them under this umbrella; keep #642 open until the full tracked scope is merged or split.

references.title: Issue #642: tracking: framework maintainability, rule ownership, and determinism codification (PR #401)
PR #401: feat(coding): codebase hygiene, DDD framework structure, and determinism design
